name: Katillotxu: A Look into the Megalithic Past Trail
name_original: Dolmen de Katillotxu — circular desde Mundaka por Sukarrieta
description: Hike a moderate 6.0-mile (9.7 km) circular route from Mundaka to the ancient Katillotxu Dolmen with stunning Urdaibai estuary views.
region_intro: Hiking around Mundaca offers diverse routes within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO-protected area. The region is characterized by a unique blend of coastal and wetland ecosystems, where the Oka River forms the Urdaibai estuary. Hikers can explore landscapes featuring high cliffs, sandy beaches, and Cantabrian woods of Holm Oak and Strawberry Trees. This rich natural environment provides varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Mundaca The most popular hiking route is Katillotxu: A Look into the Megalithic Past Trail, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the ancient Katillotxu Dolmen and provides stunning views of the Urdaibai estuary. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mundaka's narrow streets – View of Bermeo Harbor loop from Mundaka, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This route explores the town's historic quarter and offers panoramic views of Bermeo Harbor. Local hikers also love the Santa Catalina Peninsula – Charming harbor viewpoint loop from Sukarrieta, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through the Santa Catalina Peninsula, often completed in about 1 hour 34 minutes. Hiking around Mundaca is defined by its estuary, high cliffs, and diverse woodlands within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Mundaca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore Mundaca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Mundaca?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Mundaca, catering to various skill levels. The region offers a diverse network of routes within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging excursions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Mundaca?
A: Hiking in Mundaca features a unique blend of coastal and wetland ecosystems. You'll encounter high cliffs, sandy beaches, and Cantabrian woods of Holm Oak and Strawberry Trees. The terrain varies from easily accessible paths to more challenging sections requiring good fitness, all within the diverse landscape of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Mundaca?
A: Yes, Mundaca offers several easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40334107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Peninsula – Charming harbor viewpoint loop from Sukarrieta&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6-mile (5.8 km) trail that provides charming harbor viewpoints and panoramic sea vistas.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can discover while hiking in Mundaca?
A: Mundaca's trails offer access to several interesting landmarks and stunning viewpoints. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, which stands strategically at the estuary's entrance. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4687497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia (276 m)&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint or offer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mundaca?
A: Yes, many of Mundaca's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327619333/katillotxu-a-look-into-the-megalithic-past-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Katillotxu: A Look into the Megalithic Past Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.0-mile (9.7 km) circular route that leads to the ancient Katillotxu Dolmen.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Mundaca?
A: The hiking routes in Mundaca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning views of the Urdaibai estuary, and the blend of coastal and woodland environments that make each trail unique.

Q: Are Mundaca's hiking trails suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, Mundaca offers several trails that are suitable for families. The varied network includes easy paths that are less strenuous, allowing families to enjoy the natural beauty of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve together. Always check the specific trail's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Mundaca?
A: Many trails in the Mundaca region are dog-friendly, allowing you to explore the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially within the protected Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mundaca?
A: Mundaca offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly popular, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Autumn also brings the famous left-hand wave, attracting surfers, which can add to the lively atmosphere. Even winter hikes can be rewarding, offering a different perspective of the coastal and wetland landscapes.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer views of Bermeo Harbor?
A: Yes, you can enjoy panoramic views of Bermeo Harbor on certain hikes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40334123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mundaka's narrow streets – View of Bermeo Harbor loop from Mundaka&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.3-mile (8.6 km) path that explores the town's historic quarter and provides excellent vistas of the neighboring harbor.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Mundaca?
A: Mundaca, being a popular destination, generally offers parking options within or near the town center, especially close to trailheads. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the starting point of the trail on komoot for detailed information on nearby parking facilities, which may include public car parks or designated roadside spots.

Q: Are there any hikes that explore the historic parts of Mundaca?
A: Absolutely. You can immerse yourself in Mundaca's history and charm by taking a walk through its picturesque port and historic quarter. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31905671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mundaka's narrow streets – San Antonio Beach loop from Mundaka&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.9-mile (7.9 km) route that allows you to explore the town's vibrant heart and its traditional Basque-style houses.
tips: Mundaka is a picturesque coastal town located in the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. Its places of interest include a colorful old town next to the port and the beautiful hermitage of Santa Catalina, located on a wild cape with beautiful views of the sea and cliffs.
The Katillotxu dolmen is a megalithic monument consisting of a funerary chamber of which several slabs are preserved. It dates from the Neolithic period, although the exact origin is unknown.
A small mountain, 332 metres high, from which we can have a view over the mouth of the Mundaka estuary.
Crossroads correctly indicated on a post, through which we can go, through different paths to Munitiz, Sukarrieta, Goierri or the Sorbituaga-Arriatara monolith.
This Gothic-Renaissance style building was rebuilt in the 16th century, although in the 11th century there was already evidence of this parish.

This beautiful, although small, hermitage has incredible views over the estuary.
Although the current one is from the end of the s. XIX, one already existed at this point in the Middle Ages.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Dolmen de Katillotxu circular trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered of moderate difficulty. While much of the route is manageable, be prepared for a sustained and rather steep climb (pronunciada cuesta) as you ascend to the summit of Katillotxu. The total elevation gain is around 324 meters.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The hike covers a distance of approximately 9.7 kilometers. Most hikers complete the circular route in about 2 to 3 hours, depending on their pace and how long they spend exploring the sights.

faqs.question: Where can I park when hiking this trail from Mundaka or Sukarrieta?
faqs.answer: You can start this circular route from either Mundaka or Sukarrieta. Both towns offer parking options. In Mundaka, look for parking near the harbor or the town center. In Sukarrieta, parking is available, particularly closer to the coastal areas. The trail is well-marked with PR-BI 176 signs, making it easy to find the trailhead from either location.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You'll encounter the significant Katillotxu Dolmen, an ancient megalithic complex. Other highlights include the picturesque Santa Catalina Hermitage and Mundaka Harbor, stunning views from Arribieta, and the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve trail junction signpost. You'll also pass traditional Basque farmhouses, an eucalyptus forest, and the 16th-century Munitiz tower house.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: Prepare for extraordinary panoramic views of the Urdaibai estuary, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. From various vantage points, you can admire landmarks like Laida beach, the iconic Ogoño rock, and the peak of Atxarre, all framed by lush holm oak forests and the Cantabrian Sea.

faqs.question: Is the Dolmen de Katillotxu trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near archaeological sites like the dolmens, and to follow all local regulations regarding pet waste and control.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable. Winter hikes are possible, but be mindful of potentially wetter and muddier sections.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike in the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or access the general areas of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. However, always respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and follow any posted regulations to help preserve this protected area.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route primarily follows the well-marked PR-BI 176 trail. It also intersects with sections of the GR 38 and other local paths such as Katillotxu. Begirada bat iragan megalitikoari, Bizkaiko bira, and Santa Kataliña kalea, offering possibilities for longer or alternative explorations.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to Mundaka or Sukarrieta?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Mundaka and Sukarrieta are accessible by public transport, making it easy to reach the trailheads. You can typically find train or bus connections from larger cities like Bilbao to these coastal towns, allowing for a convenient start to your hike.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants near the trail in Mundaka or Sukarrieta?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Both Mundaka and Sukarrieta are charming coastal towns with various options for refreshments and meals. In Mundaka, you'll find cafes and restaurants, especially near the harbor. Sukarrieta also offers places to eat and pubs, perfect for a post-hike treat.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A camera is also a great idea to capture the stunning views.
